title: Data Analytics for Decision Makers
description: A business-focused course that helps leaders and managers translate data into better decisions. Participants learn to frame analytical questions, interpret dashboards, evaluate insights, and communicate recommendations that drive measurable outcomes.
whoShouldAttend: Ideal for managers, team leaders, business analysts, functional heads, and professionals who use reports and data outputs to make operational or strategic decisions.
outcomes: Define business problems in ways that can be tested with data.
Interpret key metrics, trends, and patterns with stronger confidence.
Use practical frameworks to avoid common analytical bias and errors.
Evaluate dashboard quality and insight relevance for leadership reporting.
Communicate data stories clearly to technical and non-technical audiences.
Prioritise actions from insights and track impact over time.

topics.title: Analytics Foundations for Managers
bullets: Types of data and selecting the right metric for each objective
Descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics
Defining useful KPIs and avoiding vanity metrics

topics.title: Insight Generation and Decision Quality
bullets: Trend analysis, segmentation, and cohort thinking
Recognising bias, outliers, and weak inference
Turning insights into decision options and trade-off discussions

topics.title: Visualisation and Data Communication
bullets: Designing executive dashboards for clarity and speed
Storytelling with data for stakeholder alignment
Presenting recommendations with evidence and confidence

topics.title: Applying Analytics in Operations
bullets: Use cases across finance, sales, HR, and operations
Simple experimentation and performance tracking cycles
Building a data-informed culture within teams
